briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. Welcome to the climate briefing for March 4, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Impacts of the Climate Crisis and International Warnings
sections.content: The impacts of global climate change are intensifying, threatening our lives and environment in various forms. According to one study, 77% of U.S. national parks have been found to be highly vulnerable to climate change [115], and the Alpine region is also reported to be experiencing a crisis due to climate change [114]. In particular, Greenlandic fishermen are facing severe threats to their livelihoods due to rising sea temperatures [116, 119, 121, 122, 123, 124], and in the UK, ecological disturbances such as a sharp decline in moth populations have been observed [127]. Domestically, the need for awareness and response to the climate crisis is being emphasized, with a bill proposed to investigate the impact of climate change on public health and develop countermeasures [1].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Carbon Neutrality Policies and Citizen Participation
sections.content: The Republic of Korea is actively pursuing various policies and citizen participation programs to achieve its carbon neutrality goals. The Happy City Construction Agency (Haengbokcheong) is holding a '2040 Carbon Neutrality Hero' youth competition to mark its 20th anniversary, and Gwangmyeong City is fostering 'Youth Climate Soldiers,' among other educational and participatory activities aimed at strengthening the climate leadership of future generations [12, 13, 14, 19, 22, 34, 39, 40, 41, 42, 43, 48, 49, 55, 61, 64, 67, 74, 75]. Several local governments are operating a carbon neutrality points system, offering incentives of up to 400,000 KRW for energy-saving activities. Hadong County is recruiting new participants for 2026, and Daejeon's Daedeok-gu is recruiting participants for the vehicle carbon neutrality points system on a first-come, first-served basis, thereby encouraging active citizen participation [6, 17, 18, 27, 28, 29, 30, 33, 36, 37, 38, 50, 53, 59, 68, 69, 70, 71, 72]. Furthermore, Chungnam Province has embarked on building resident-led carbon-neutral village communities, and Wonju Camp Long Citizens' Park is practicing carbon neutrality through an 'eco-friendly energy source transition' [15, 16, 60, 76, 77], promoting climate action across the entire local community. Park Eun-sik, head of the Korea Forest Service, emphasized that 'tree planting is carbon neutrality' and announced plans to declare a nationwide tree-planting campaign in 2026 [23].

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic Climate Technology and Industrial Cooperation
sections.content: The domestic industry is strengthening climate technology innovation and public-private cooperation with the goal of achieving 'growth-oriented carbon neutrality'. The 'Climate Tech Innovation Alliance' has been launched, strengthening public-private communication and fully embarking on fostering new carbon-neutral industries [7, 10, 11, 20, 25, 26, 31, 32, 47, 51, 56, 57, 62, 63, 65, 73]. The Korea Industrial Complex Corporation (KICOX) held a briefing session to support the carbon-neutral transition of industrial complexes, offering an exceptional interest rate of around 1% on 80 billion KRW, thereby encouraging companies to transition to eco-friendly practices [35]. Ilji Tech Co., Ltd. achieved the highest rating in the CDP Climate Change Response assessment, leading the automotive parts industry's response to climate change [4]. Furthermore, the upcoming Korea Window, Door, Awning & Glass Fair in March will prominently feature technologies for carbon neutrality and zero-energy building [44]. The Association of Environmental Research Institute Directors is transitioning to the 'climate and energy' sector and facilitating carbon-neutral technology exchange [45], thereby strengthening climate response capabilities across the industry.

sections.title: 🌱 Expansion of Renewable Energy and Technological Advancements
sections.content: In the renewable energy sector, positive progress is being made towards wider adoption through the introduction of innovative technologies and policy support. Notably, the official recognition of heat from the air as renewable energy and the decision to invest 14.5 billion KRW in national funds for heat pump dissemination have significantly boosted the spread of eco-friendly heating systems [78, 81, 83, 86, 94, 98, 101, 103, 105]. The Ministry of Agriculture, Food and Rural Affairs and the Ministry of Climate have signed an MOU to utilize livestock manure as renewable energy, cooperating to enhance rural sustainability [90, 100]. Lotte Fine Chemical has commercially introduced green ammonia produced entirely from renewable energy sources [91, 109]. Domestic and international companies are actively pursuing various renewable energy projects, including recruiting partners for solar rooftop development [96, 102, 106, 108] and signing MOUs for wind power projects [97, 107]. Furthermore, in the Honam region, 470MW of renewable energy is participating in quasi-centralized dispatch, establishing itself as a primary energy source [79]. Construction of the Gunsan Offshore Wind Power Industry Support Center has also resumed, expected to contribute to the expansion of offshore wind power [111].

sections.title: ⚠️ Challenges in Renewable Energy and International Policy Changes
sections.content: Despite efforts to expand renewable energy, some challenges and international policy changes are being observed. Domestically, concerns have been raised that the suspension of the Gunsan Saemangeum ESS (Energy Storage System) demonstration project is out of step with the government's stance on expanding renewable energy [82, 84]. In this regard, the high variability of renewable energy was pointed out, and the necessity of introducing seasonal and time-based distribution was emphasized [112]. Internationally, there have been notable shifts in the direction of climate policy, such as the European Union (EU) abruptly removing the steel emissions labeling system from its Net-Zero Industry Act [110]. These challenges suggest the need for thorough review and flexible responses during the renewable energy transition, illustrating the complex nature of climate change response.

briefing.closing: As discussed in today's briefing, the impacts of the climate crisis are intensifying globally. However, policy efforts and technological innovations to achieve carbon neutrality and expand renewable energy are actively underway both domestically and internationally. We look forward to bringing you more in-depth news in our next briefing. This concludes today's climate briefing.
